abstractCompactExhaustionStatus.label: abstract compact-source exhaustion theorem
abstractCompactExhaustionStatus.reason: The Hilbert-space Mosco/compact-source/min-max implication is closed.  It is conditional only on the continuum trace-frame lower bound needed to identify the sampled closed-trace Galerkin spaces with H_M.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

moscoLimsupStatus.label: Mosco limsup/recovery sequence
moscoLimsupStatus.reason: The compact-source proof gives the recovery sequence once the sampled trace map has a continuum uniform lower frame bound and bounded correction right inverse.

moscoLiminfStatus.label: Mosco liminf/compactness
moscoLiminfStatus.reason: The compact-source proof gives the weak compactness/trace-closure argument once sampled trace quadrature is known to converge on A-bounded high-block sequences.

ellipticTraceStatus.label: commuted Sturm/Volterra elliptic estimate
ellipticTraceStatus.reason: The compact-source exhaustion proof supplies the valid replacement for the invalid raw compact-kernel Sobolev route.

sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.label: source operator norm convergence
sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.reason: The compact-source proof shows that Mosco convergence of the closed high blocks plus compactness of S=Ehat^*Ehat gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

spectralProjectionConvergenceStatus.label: Riesz spectral projection convergence
spectralProjectionConvergenceStatus.reason: Standard consequence of ||P_N S P_N-S||_{A->A}->0 and an open gap around the top-two source spectrum.

minmaxPassageStatus.label: min-max inequality passage
minmaxPassageStatus.reason: Once source operator norm convergence and spectral projection convergence hold, the Courant-Fischer/Riesz projection argument passes the normalized tail inequality to the continuum.

formalProof: Define H_A as the Hilbert completion under the positive Volterra/Sturm form A, N=ker R_global, and H_M=N cap L_M^{perp_A}.
Let V_N be the polynomial Galerkin space and H_{M,N}=V_N cap ker R_N cap L_{M,N}^{perp_A}.  Mosco convergence H_{M,N}->H_M is the required exhaustion theorem.

The source row is the direct Lagrange/adjoint pair E_u f=(B_P[h_u,f](s0), (P^*h_u)(s0)f(s0)).  The source-range Hardy/Green theorem constructs its A-Riesz representers uniformly, so S=E^*E is compact in the A-Hilbert source model.
A-graph Galerkin convergence plus compact direct source-row factorization gives ||P_NSP_N-S||_{A->A}->0.
Riesz projection convergence then gives P_{2,N}->P_2, and the normalized finite min-max tail inequality passes to H_M cap ker R_global by recovery sequences and lower limits.
interpretation: The functional-analytic compact-source passage is proved, and the continuum trace-frame lower-bound theorem supplies the missing bounded correction right inverse.  The normalized tail estimate now passes to the full closed high block.
